planned change for an instance object into the set of\n542: // global planned changes.\n543: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) writeChange(ctx EvalContext, change *plans.ResourceInstanceChange, deposedKey states.DeposedKey) error {\n544: \tchanges := ctx.Changes()\n545: \n546: \tif change == nil {\n547: \t\t// Caller sets nil to indicate that we need to remove a change from\n548: \t\t// the set of changes.\n549: \t\tchanges.RemoveResourceInstanceChange(n.Addr, deposedKey)\n550: \t\treturn nil\n551: \t}\n552: \n553: \tif change.Addr.String() != n.Addr.String() || change.DeposedKey != deposedKey {\n554: \t\t// Should never happen, and indicates a bug in the caller.\n555: \t\tpanic(\"inconsistent address and/or deposed key in writeChange\")\n556: \t}\n557: \tif change.PrevRunAddr.Resource.Resource.Type == \"\" {\n558: \t\t// Should never happen, and indicates a bug in the caller.\n559: \t\t// (The change.Encode function actually has its own fixup to just\n560: \t\t// quietly make this match change.Addr in the incorrect case, but we\n561: \t\t// intentionally panic here in order to catch incorrect callers where\n562: \t\t// the stack trace will hopefully be actually useful. The tolerance\n563: \t\t// at the next layer down is mainly to accommodate sloppy input in\n564: \t\t// older tests.)\n565: \t\tpanic(\"unpopulated ResourceInstanceChange.PrevRunAddr in writeChange\")\n566: \t}\n567: \n568: \tchanges.AppendResourceInstanceChange(change)\n569: \tif deposedKey == states.NotDeposed {\n570: \t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] writeChange: recorded %s change for %s\", change.Action, n.Addr)\n571: \t} else {\n572: \t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] writeChange: recorded %s change for %s deposed object %s\", change.Action, n.Addr, deposedKey)\n573: \t}\n574: \n575: \treturn nil\n576: }\n577: \n578: // refresh does a refresh for a resource\n579: // if the second return value is non-nil, the refresh is deferred\n580: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) refresh(ctx EvalContext, deposedKey states.DeposedKey, state *states.ResourceInstanceObject, deferralAllowed bool) (*states.ResourceInstanceObject, *providers.Deferred, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n581: \tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n582: \tvar deferred *providers.Deferred\n583: \tabsAddr := n.Addr\n584: \tif deposedKey == states.NotDeposed {\n585: \t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] NodeAbstractResourceInstance.refresh for %s\", absAddr)\n586: \t} else {\n587: \t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] NodeAbstractResourceInstance.refresh for %s (deposed object %s)\", absAddr, deposedKey)\n588: \t}\n589: \tprovider, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n590: \tif err != nil {\n591: \t\treturn state, deferred, diags.Append(err)\n592: \t}\n593: \t// If we have no state, we don't do any refreshing\n594: \tif state == nil {\n595: \t\tlog.Printf(\"[DEBUG] refresh: %s: no state, so not refreshing\", absAddr)\n596: \t\treturn state, deferred, diags\n597: \t}\n598: \n599: \tschema, _ := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceAddr(n.Addr.Resource.ContainingResource())\n600: \tif schema == nil {\n601: \t\t// Should be caught during validation, so we don't bother with a pretty error here\n602: \t\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\"provider does not support resource type %q\", n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Type))\n603: \t\treturn state, deferred, diags\n604: \t}\n605: \n606: \tmetaConfigVal, metaDiags := n.providerMetas(ctx)\n607: \tdiags = diags.Append(metaDiags)\n608: \tif diags.HasErrors() {\n609: \t\treturn state, deferred, diags\n610: \t}\n611: \n612: \t// Call pre-refresh hook\n613: \tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\n614: \t\treturn h.PreRefresh(n.HookResourceIdentity(), deposedKey, state.Value)\n615: \t}))\n616: \tif diags.HasErrors() {\n617: \t\treturn state, deferred, diags\n618: \t}\n619: \n620: \t// Refresh!\n621: \tpriorVal := state.Value\n622: \n623: \t// Unmarked before sending to provider\n624: \tvar priorMarks []cty.PathValueMarks\n625: \tpriorVal, priorMarks = priorVal.UnmarkDeepWithPaths()\n626: \n627: \tvar resp providers.ReadResourceResponse\n628: \tif n.override != nil {\n629: \t\t// If we have an override set for this resource, we don't want to talk\n630: \t\t// to the provider so we'll just return whatever was in state.\n631: \t\tresp = providers.ReadResourceResponse{\n632: \t\t\tNewState: priorVal,\n633: \t\t}\n634: \t} else {\n635: \t\tresp = provider.ReadResource(providers.ReadResourceRequest{\n636: \t\t\tTypeName:     n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Type,\n637: \t\t\tPriorState:   priorVal,\n638: \t\t\tPrivate:      state.Private,\n639: \t\t\tProviderMeta: metaConfigVal,\n640: \t\t\tClientCapabilities: providers.ClientCapabilities{\n641: \t\t\t\tDeferralAllowed: deferralAllowed,\n642: \t\t\t},\n643: \t\t})\n644: \n645: \t\t// If we don't support deferrals, but the provider reports a deferral and does not\n646: \t\t// emit any error level diagnostics, we should emit an error.\n647: \t\tif resp.Deferred != nil && !deferralAllowed && !resp.Diagnostics.HasErrors() {\n648: \t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(deferring.UnexpectedProviderDeferralDiagnostic(n.Addr))\n649: \t\t}\n650: \n651: \t\tif resp.Deferred != nil {\n652: \t\t\tdeferred = resp.Deferred\n653: \t\t}\n654: \t}\n655: \tif n.Config != nil {\n656: \t\tresp.Diagnostics = resp.Diagnostics.InConfigBody(n.Config.Config, n.Addr.String())\n657: \t}\n658: \n659: \tdiags = diags.Append(resp.Diagnostics)\n660: \tif diags.HasErrors() {\n661: \t\treturn state, deferred, diags\n662: \t}\n663: \n664: \tif resp.NewState == cty.NilVal {\n665: \t\t// This ought not to happen in real cases since it's not possible to\n666: \t\t// send NilVal over the plugin RPC channel, but it can come up in\n667: \t\t// tests due to sloppy mocking.\n668: \t\tpanic(\"new state is cty.NilVal\")\n669: \t}\n670: \n671: \t// If we have deferred the refresh, we expect the new state not to be wholly known\n672: \t// and callers should be prepared to handle this.\n673: \tif !resp.NewState.IsWhollyKnown() && deferred == nil {\n674: \t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n675: \t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n676: \t\t\t\"Provider produced invalid object\",\n677: \t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n678: \t\t\t\t\"Provider %q planned an invalid value for %s during refresh: %s.\\n\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\",\n679: \t\t\t\tn.ResolvedProvider.Provider, absAddr, \"The returned state contains unknown values\",\n680: \t\t\t),\n681: \t\t))\n682: \t}\n683: \n684: \tfor _, err := range resp.NewState.Type().TestConformance(schema.ImpliedType()) {\n685: \t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n686: \t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n687: \t\t\t\"Provider produced invalid object\",\n688: \t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n689: \t\t\t\t\"Provider %q planned an invalid value for %s during refresh: %s.\\n\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\",\n690: \t\t\t\tn.ResolvedProvider.Provider, absAddr, tfdiags.FormatError(err),\n691: \t\t\t),\n692: \t\t))\n693: \t}\n694: \tif diags.HasErrors() {\n695: \t\treturn state, deferred, diags\n696: \t}\n697: \n698: \tnewState := objchange.NormalizeObjectFromLegacySDK(resp.NewState, schema)\n699: \tif !newState.RawEquals(resp.NewState) {\n700: \t\t// We had to fix up this object in some way, and we still need to\n701: \t\t// accept any changes for compatibility, so all we can do is log a\n702: \t\t// warning about the change.\n703: \t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] Provider %q produced an invalid new value containing null blocks for %q during refresh\\n\", n.ResolvedProvider.Provider, n.Addr)\n704: \t}\n705: \n706: \tret := state.DeepCopy()\n707: \tret.Value = newState\n708: \tret.Private = resp.Private\n709: \n710: \t// We have no way to exempt provider using the legacy SDK from this check,\n711: \t// so we can only log inconsistencies with the updated state values.\n712: \t// In most cases these are not errors anyway, and represent \"drift\" from\n713: \t// external changes which will be handled by the subsequent plan.\n714: \tif errs := objchange.AssertObjectCompatible(schema, priorVal, ret.Value); len(errs) > 0 {\n715: \t\tvar buf strings.Builder\n716: \t\tfmt.Fprintf(&buf, \"[WARN] Provider %q produced an unexpected new value for %s during refresh.\", n.ResolvedProvider.Provider.String(), absAddr)\n717: \t\tfor _, err := range errs {\n718: \t\t\tfmt.Fprintf(&buf, \"\\n      - %s\", tfdiags.FormatError(err))\n719: \t\t}\n720: \t\tlog.Print(buf.String())\n721: \t}\n722: \n723: \t// Call post-refresh hook\n724: \tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\n725: \t\treturn h.PostRefresh(n.HookResourceIdentity(), deposedKey, priorVal, ret.Value)\n726: \t}))\n727: \tif diags.HasErrors() {\n728: \t\treturn ret, deferred, diags\n729: \t}\n730: \n731: \t// Mark the value with any prior marks from the state, and the marks from\n732: \t// the schema. This ensures we capture any marks from the last\n733: \t// configuration, as well as any marks from the schema which were not in\n734: \t// the prior state. New marks may appear when the prior state was from an\n735: \t// import operation, or if the provider added new marks to the schema.\n736: \tret.Value = ret.Value.MarkWithPaths(priorMarks)\n737: \tif moreSensitivePaths := schema.SensitivePaths(ret.Value, nil); len(moreSensitivePaths) != 0 {\n738: \t\tret.Value = marks.MarkPaths(ret.Value, marks.Sensitive, moreSensitivePaths)\n739: \t}\n740: \n741: \treturn ret, deferred, diags\n742: }\n743: \n744: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) plan(\n745: \tctx EvalContext,\n746: \tplannedChange *plans.ResourceInstanceChange,\n747: \tcurrentState *states.ResourceInstanceObject,\n748: \tcreateBeforeDestroy bool,\n749: \tforceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance,\n750: ) (*plans.ResourceInstanceChange, *states.ResourceInstanceObject, *providers.Deferred, instances.RepetitionData, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n751: \tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n752: \tvar keyData instances.RepetitionData\n753: \tvar deferred *providers.Deferred\n754: \n755: \tresource := n.Addr.Resource.Resource\n756: \tdeferralAllowed := ctx.Deferrals().DeferralAllowed()\n757: \n758: \tprovider, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n759: \tif err != nil {\n760: \t\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags.Append(err)\n761: \t}\n762: \n763: \tschema, _ := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceAddr(resource)\n764: \tif schema == nil {\n765: \t\t// Should be caught during validation, so we don't bother with a pretty error here\n766: \t\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\"provider does not support resource type %q\", resource.Type))\n767: \t\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags\n768: \t}\n769: \n770: \t// If we're importing and generating config, generate it now.\n771: \tif n.Config == nil {\n772: \t\t// This shouldn't happen. A node that isn't generating config should\n773: \t\t// have embedded config, and the rest of Terraform should enforce this.\n774: \t\t// If, however, we didn't do things correctly the next line will panic,\n775: \t\t// so let's not do that and return an error message with more context.\n776: \n777: \t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n778: \t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n779: \t\t\t\"Resource has no configuration\",\n780: \t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Terraform attempted to process a resource at %s that has no configuration. This is a bug in Terraform; please report it!\", n.Addr.String())))\n781: \t\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags\n782: \t}\n783: \n784: \tconfig := *n.Config\n785: \n786: \tcheckRuleSeverity := tfdiags.Error\n787: \tif n.preDestroyRefresh {\n788: \t\tcheckRuleSeverity = tfdiags.Warning\n789: \t}\n790: \n791: \tif plannedChange != nil {\n792: \t\t// If we already planned the action, we stick to that plan\n793: \t\tcreateBeforeDestroy = plannedChange.Action == plans.CreateThenDelete\n794: \t}\n795: \n796: \t// Evaluate the configuration\n797: \tforEach, _, _ := evaluateForEachExpression(n.Config.ForEach, ctx, false)\n798: \n799: \tkeyData = EvalDataForInstanceKey(n.ResourceInstanceAddr().Resource.Key, forEach)\n800: \n801: \tcheckDiags := evalCheckRules(\n802: \t\taddrs.ResourcePrecondition,\n803: \t\tn.Config.Preconditions,\n804: \t\tctx, n.Addr, keyData,\n805: \t\tcheckRuleSeverity,\n806: \t)\n807: \tdiags = diags.Append(checkDiags)\n808: \tif diags.HasErrors() {\n809: \t\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags // failed preconditions prevent further evaluation\n810: \t}\n811: \n812: \t// If we have a previous plan and the action was a noop, then the only\n813: \t// reason we're in this method was to evaluate the preconditions. There's\n814: \t// no need to re-plan this resource.\n815: \tif plannedChange != nil && plannedChange.Action == plans.NoOp {\n816: \t\treturn plannedChange, currentState.DeepCopy(), deferred, keyData, diags\n817: \t}\n818: \n819: \torigConfigVal, _, configDiags := ctx.EvaluateBlock(config.Config, schema, nil, keyData)\n820: \tdiags = diags.Append(configDiags)\n821: \tdiags = diags.Append(\n822: \t\tvalidateResourceForbiddenEphemeralValues(ctx, origConfigVal, schema).InConfigBody(n.Config.Config, n.Addr.String()),\n823: \t)\n824: \tif diags.HasErrors() {\n825: \t\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags\n826: \t}\n827: \n828: \tmetaConfigVal, metaDiags := n.providerMetas(ctx)\n829: \tdiags = diags.Append(metaDiags)\n830: \tif diags.HasErrors() {\n831: \t\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags\n832: \t}\n833: \n834: \tvar priorVal cty.Value\n835: \tvar priorValTainted cty.Value\n836: \tvar priorPrivate []byte\n837: \tif currentState != nil {\n838: \t\tif currentState.Status != states.ObjectTainted {\n839: \t\t\tpriorVal = currentState.Value\n840: \t\t\tpriorPrivate = currentState.Private\n841: \t\t} else {\n842: \t\t\t// If the prior state is tainted then we'll proceed below like\n843: \t\t\t// we're creating an entirely new object, but then turn it into\n844: \t\t\t// a synthetic \"Replace\" change at the end, creating the same\n845: \t\t\t// result as if the provider had marked at least one argument\n846: \t\t\t// change as \"requires replacement\".\n847: \t\t\tpriorValTainted = currentState.Value\n848: \t\t\tpriorVal = cty.NullVal(schema.ImpliedType())\n849: \t\t}\n850: \t} else {\n851: \t\tpriorVal = cty.NullVal(schema.ImpliedType())\n852: \t}\n853: \n854: \tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] Re-validating config for %q\", n.Addr)\n855: \t// Allow the provider to validate the final set of values.  The config was\n856: \t// statically validated early on, but there may have been unknown values\n857: \t// which the provider could not validate at the time.\n858: \t//\n859: \t// TODO: It would be more correct to validate the config after\n860: \t// ignore_changes has been applied, but the current implementation cannot\n861: \t// exclude computed-only attributes when given the `all` option.\n862: \n863: \t// we must unmark and use the original config, since the ignore_changes\n864: \t// handling below needs access to the marks.\n865: \tunmarkedConfigVal, _ := origConfigVal.UnmarkDeep()\n866: \tvalidateResp := provider.ValidateResourceConfig(\n867: \t\tproviders.ValidateResourceConfigRequest{\n868: \t\t\tTypeName: n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Type,\n869: \t\t\tConfig:   unmarkedConfigVal,\n870: \t\t},\n871: \t)\n872: \tdiags = diags.Append(validateResp.Diagnostics.InConfigBody(config.Config, n.Addr.String()))\n873: \tif diags.HasErrors() {\n874: \t\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags\n875: \t}\n876: \n877: \t// ignore_changes is meant to only apply to the configuration, so it must\n878: \t// be applied before we generate a plan. This ensures the config used for\n879: \t// the proposed value, the proposed value itself, and the config presented\n880: \t// to the provider in the PlanResourceChange request all agree on the\n881: \t// starting values.\n882: \t// Here we operate on the marked values, so as to revert any changes to the\n883: \t// marks as well as the value.\n884: \tconfigValIgnored, ignoreChangeDiags := n.processIgnoreChanges(priorVal, origConfigVal, schema)\n885: \tdiags = diags.Append(ignoreChangeDiags)\n886: \tif ignoreChangeDiags.HasErrors() {\n887: \t\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags\n888: \t}\n889: \n890: \t// Create an unmarked version of our config val and our prior val.\n891: \t// Store the paths for the config val to re-mark after we've sent things\n892: \t// over the wire.\n893: \tunmarkedConfigVal, unmarkedPaths := configValIgnored.UnmarkDeepWithPaths()\n894: \tunmarkedPriorVal, _ := priorVal.UnmarkDeepWithPaths()\n895: \n896: \tproposedNewVal := objchange.ProposedNew(schema, unmarkedPriorVal, unmarkedConfigVal)\n897: \n898: \t// Call pre-diff hook\n899: \tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\n900: \t\treturn h.PreDiff(n.HookResourceIdentity(), addrs.NotDeposed, priorVal, proposedNewVal)\n901: \t}))\n902: \tif diags.HasErrors() {\n903: \t\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags\n904: \t}\n905: \n906: \tvar resp providers.PlanResourceChangeResponse\n907: \tif n.override != nil {\n908: \t\t// Then we have an override to apply for this change. But, overrides\n909: \t\t// only matter when we are creating a resource for the first time as we\n910: \t\t// only apply computed values.\n911: \t\tif priorVal.IsNull() {\n912: \t\t\t// Then we are actually creating something, so let's populate the\n913: \t\t\t// computed values from our override value.\n914: \t\t\toverride, overrideDiags := mocking.PlanComputedValuesForResource(proposedNewVal, schema)\n915: \t\t\tresp = providers.PlanResourceChangeResponse{\n916: \t\t\t\tPlannedState: override,\n917: \t\t\t\tDiagnostics:  overrideDiags,\n918: \t\t\t}\n919: \t\t} else {\n920: \t\t\t// This is an update operation, and we don't actually have any\n921: \t\t\t// computed values that need to be applied.\n922: \t\t\tresp = providers.PlanResourceChangeResponse{\n923: \t\t\t\tPlannedState: proposedNewVal,\n924: \t\t\t}\n925: \t\t}\n926: \t} else {\n927: \t\tresp = provider.PlanResourceChange(providers.PlanResourceChangeRequest{\n928: \t\t\tTypeName:         n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Type,\n929: \t\t\tConfig:           unmarkedConfigVal,\n930: \t\t\tPriorState:       unmarkedPriorVal,\n931: \t\t\tProposedNewState: proposedNewVal,\n932: \t\t\tPriorPrivate:     priorPrivate,\n933: \t\t\tProviderMeta:     metaConfigVal,\n934: \t\t\tClientCapabilities: providers.ClientCapabilities{\n935: \t\t\t\tDeferralAllowed: deferralAllowed,\n936: \t\t\t},\n937: \t\t})\n938: \t\t// If we don't support deferrals, but the provider reports a deferral and does not\n939: \t\t// emit any error level diagnostics, we should emit an error.\n940: \t\tif resp.Deferred != nil && !deferralAllowed && !resp.Diagnostics.HasErrors() {\n941: \t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(deferring.UnexpectedProviderDeferralDiagnostic(n.Addr))\n942: \t\t}\n943: \t}\n944: \tdiags = diags.Append(resp.Diagnostics.InConfigBody(config.Config, n.Addr.String()))\n945: \tif diags.HasErrors() {\n946: \t\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags\n947: \t}\n948: \n949: \t// We mark this node as deferred at a later point when we know the complete change\n950: \tif resp.Deferred != nil {\n951: \t\tdeferred = resp.Deferred\n952: \t}\n953: \n954: \tplannedNewVal := resp.PlannedState\n955: \tplannedPrivate := resp.PlannedPrivate\n956: \n957: \t// These checks are only relevant if the provider is not deferring the\n958: \t// change.\n959: \tif deferred == nil {\n960: \t\tif plannedNewVal == cty.NilVal {\n961: \t\t\t// Should never happen. Since real-world providers return via RPC a nil\n962: \t\t\t// is always a bug in the client-side stub. This is more likely caused\n963: \t\t\t// by an incompletely-configured mock provider in tests, though.\n964: \t\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"PlanResourceChange of %s produced nil value\", n.Addr))\n965: \t\t}\n966: \n967: \t\t// We allow the planned new value to disagree with configuration _values_\n968: \t\t// here, since that allows the provider to do special logic like a\n969: \t\t// DiffSuppressFunc, but we still require that the provider produces\n970: \t\t// a value whose type conforms to the schema.\n971: \t\tfor _, err := range plannedNewVal.Type().TestConformance(schema.ImpliedType()) {\n972: \t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n973: \t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n974: \t\t\t\t\"Provider produced invalid plan\",\n975: \t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n976: \t\t\t\t\t\"Provider %q planned an invalid value for %s.\\n\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\",\n977: \t\t\t\t\tn.ResolvedProvider.Provider, tfdiags.FormatErrorPrefixed(err, n.Addr.String()),\n978: \t\t\t\t),\n979: \t\t\t))\n980: \t\t}\n981: \n982: \t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n983: \t\t\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags\n984: \t\t}\n985: \n986: \t\tif errs := objchange.AssertPlanValid(schema, unmarkedPriorVal, unmarkedConfigVal, plannedNewVal); len(errs) > 0 {\n987: \t\t\tif resp.LegacyTypeSystem {\n988: \t\t\t\t// The shimming of the old type system in the legacy SDK is not precise\n989: \t\t\t\t// enough to pass this consistency check, so we'll give it a pass here,\n990: \t\t\t\t// but we will generate a warning about it so that we are more likely\n991: \t\t\t\t// to notice in the logs if an inconsistency beyond the type system\n992: \t\t\t\t// leads to a downstream provider failure.\n993: \t\t\t\tvar buf strings.Builder\n994: \t\t\t\tfmt.Fprintf(&buf,\n995: \t\t\t\t\t\"[WARN] Provider %q produced an invalid plan for %s, but we are tolerating it because it is using the legacy plugin SDK.\\n    The following problems may be the cause of any confusing errors from downstream operations:\",\n996: \t\t\t\t\tn.ResolvedProvider.Provider, n.Addr,\n997: \t\t\t\t)\n998: \t\t\t\tfor _, err := range errs {\n999: \t\t\t\t\tfmt.Fprintf(&buf, \"\\n      - %s\", tfdiags.FormatError(err))\n1000: \t\t\t\t}\n1001: \t\t\t\tlog.Print(buf.String())\n1002: \t\t\t} else {\n1003: \t\t\t\tfor _, err := range errs {\n1004: \t\t\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n1005: \t\t\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n1006: \t\t\t\t\t\t\"Provider produced invalid plan\",\n1007: \t\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n1008: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"Provider %q planned an invalid value for %s.\\n\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\",\n1009: \t\t\t\t\t\t\tn.ResolvedProvider.Provider, tfdiags.FormatErrorPrefixed(err, n.Addr.String()),\n1010: \t\t\t\t\t\t),\n1011: \t\t\t\t\t))\n1012: \t\t\t\t}\n1013: \t\t\t\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags\n1014: \t\t\t}\n1015: \t\t}\n1016: \t}\n1017: \n1018: \tif resp.LegacyTypeSystem {\n1019: \t\t// Because we allow legacy providers to depart from the contract and\n1020: \t\t// return changes to non-computed values, the plan response may have\n1021: \t\t// altered values that were already suppressed with ignore_changes.\n1022: \t\t// A prime example of this is where providers attempt to obfuscate\n1023: \t\t// config data by turning the config value into a hash and storing the\n1024: \t\t// hash value in the state. There are enough cases of this in existing\n1025: \t\t// providers that we must accommodate the behavior for now, so for\n1026: \t\t// ignore_changes to work at all on these values, we will revert the\n1027: \t\t// ignored values once more.\n1028: \t\t// A nil schema is passed to processIgnoreChanges to indicate that we\n1029: \t\t// don't want to fixup a config value according to the schema when\n1030: \t\t// ignoring \"all\", rather we are reverting provider imposed changes.\n1031: \t\tplannedNewVal, ignoreChangeDiags = n.processIgnoreChanges(unmarkedPriorVal, plannedNewVal, nil)\n1032: \t\tdiags = diags.Append(ignoreChangeDiags)\n1033: \t\tif ignoreChangeDiags.HasErrors() {\n1034: \t\t\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags\n1035: \t\t}\n1036: \t}\n1037: \n1038: \t// Add the marks back to the planned new value -- this must happen after\n1039: \t// ignore changes have been processed. We add in the schema marks as well,\n1040: \t// to ensure that provider defined private attributes are marked correctly\n1041: \t// here.\n1042: \tunmarkedPlannedNewVal := plannedNewVal\n1043: \tplannedNewVal = plannedNewVal.MarkWithPaths(unmarkedPaths)\n1044: \tif sensitivePaths := schema.SensitivePaths(plannedNewVal, nil); len(sensitivePaths) != 0 {\n1045: \t\tplannedNewVal = marks.MarkPaths(plannedNewVal, marks.Sensitive, sensitivePaths)\n1046: \t}\n1047: \n1048: \treqRep, reqRepDiags := getRequiredReplaces(unmarkedPriorVal, unmarkedPlannedNewVal, resp.RequiresReplace, n.ResolvedProvider.Provider, n.Addr)\n1049: \tdiags = diags.Append(reqRepDiags)\n1050: \tif diags.HasErrors() {\n1051: \t\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags\n1052: \t}\n1053: \n1054: \taction, actionReason := getAction(n.Addr, unmarkedPriorVal, unmarkedPlannedNewVal, createBeforeDestroy, forceReplace, reqRep)\n1055: \n1056: \tif action.IsReplace() {\n1057: \t\t// In this strange situation we want to produce a change object that\n1058: \t\t// shows our real prior object but has a _new_ object that is built\n1059: \t\t// from a null prior object, since we're going to delete the one\n1060: \t\t// that has all the computed values on it.\n1061: \t\t//\n1062: \t\t// Therefore we'll ask the provider to plan again here, giving it\n1063: \t\t// a null object for the prior, and then we'll meld that with the\n1064: \t\t// _actual_ prior state to produce a correctly-shaped replace change.\n1065: \t\t// The resulting change should show any computed attributes changing\n1066: \t\t// from known prior values to unknown values, unless the provider is\n1067: \t\t// able to predict new values for any of these computed attributes.\n1068: \t\tnullPriorVal := cty.NullVal(schema.ImpliedType())\n1069: \n1070: \t\t// Since there is no prior state to compare after replacement, we need\n1071: \t\t// a new unmarked config from our original with no ignored values.\n1072: \t\tunmarkedConfigVal := origConfigVal\n1073: \t\tif origConfigVal.ContainsMarked() {\n1074: \t\t\tunmarkedConfigVal, _ = origConfigVal.UnmarkDeep()\n1075: \t\t}\n1076: \n1077: \t\t// create a new proposed value from the null state and the config\n1078: \t\tproposedNewVal = objchange.ProposedNew(schema, nullPriorVal, unmarkedConfigVal)\n1079: \n1080: \t\tif n.override != nil {\n1081: \t\t\t// In this case, we are always creating the resource so we don't\n1082: \t\t\t// do any validation, and just call out to the mocking library.\n1083: \t\t\toverride, overrideDiags := mocking.PlanComputedValuesForResource(proposedNewVal, schema)\n1084: \t\t\tresp = providers.PlanResourceChangeResponse{\n1085: \t\t\t\tPlannedState: override,\n1086: \t\t\t\tDiagnostics:  overrideDiags,\n1087: \t\t\t}\n1088: \t\t} else {\n1089: \t\t\tresp = provider.PlanResourceChange(providers.PlanResourceChangeRequest{\n1090: \t\t\t\tTypeName:         n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Type,\n1091: \t\t\t\tConfig:           unmarkedConfigVal,\n1092: \t\t\t\tPriorState:       nullPriorVal,\n1093: \t\t\t\tProposedNewState: proposedNewVal,\n1094: \t\t\t\tPriorPrivate:     plannedPrivate,\n1095: \t\t\t\tProviderMeta:     metaConfigVal,\n1096: \t\t\t\tClientCapabilities: providers.ClientCapabilities{\n1097: \t\t\t\t\tDeferralAllowed: deferralAllowed,\n1098: \t\t\t\t},\n1099: \t\t\t})\n1100: \n1101: \t\t\t// If we don't support deferrals, but the provider reports a deferral and does not\n1102: \t\t\t// emit any error level diagnostics, we should emit an error.\n1103: \t\t\tif resp.Deferred != nil && !deferralAllowed && !resp.Diagnostics.HasErrors() {\n1104: \t\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(deferring.UnexpectedProviderDeferralDiagnostic(n.Addr))\n1105: \t\t\t}\n1106: \t\t}\n1107: \t\t// We need to tread carefully here, since if there are any warnings\n1108: \t\t// in here they probably also came out of our previous call to\n1109: \t\t// PlanResourceChange above, and so we don't want to repeat them.\n1110: \t\t// Consequently, we break from the usual pattern here and only\n1111: \t\t// append these new diagnostics if there's at least one error inside.\n1112: \t\tif resp.Diagnostics.HasErrors() {\n1113: \t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(resp.Diagnostics.InConfigBody(config.Config, n.Addr.String()))\n1114: \t\t\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags\n1115: \t\t}\n1116: \n1117: \t\tif deferred == nil && resp.Deferred != nil {\n1118: \t\t\tdeferred = resp.Deferred\n1119: \t\t}\n1120: \n1121: \t\tplannedNewVal = resp.PlannedState\n1122: \t\tplannedPrivate = resp.PlannedPrivate\n1123: \n1124: \t\tif len(unmarkedPaths) > 0 {\n1125: \t\t\tplannedNewVal = plannedNewVal.MarkWithPaths(unmarkedPaths)\n1126: \t\t}\n1127: \n1128: \t\tfor _, err := range plannedNewVal.Type().TestConformance(schema.ImpliedType()) {\n1129: \t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n1130: \t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n1131: \t\t\t\t\"Provider produced invalid plan\",\n1132: \t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n1133: \t\t\t\t\t\"Provider %q planned an invalid value for %s%s.\\n\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\",\n1134: \t\t\t\t\tn.ResolvedProvider.Provider, n.Addr, tfdiags.FormatError(err),\n1135: \t\t\t\t),\n1136: \t\t\t))\n1137: \t\t}\n1138: \t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n1139: \t\t\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags\n1140: \t\t}\n1141: \t}\n1142: \n1143: \t// If our prior value was tainted then we actually want this to appear\n1144: \t// as a replace change, even though so far we've been treating it as a\n1145: \t// create.\n1146: \tif action == plans.Create && !priorValTainted.IsNull() {\n1147: \t\tif createBeforeDestroy {\n1148: \t\t\taction = plans.CreateThenDelete\n1149: \t\t} else {\n1150: \t\t\taction = plans.DeleteThenCreate\n1151: \t\t}\n1152: \t\tpriorVal = priorValTainted\n1153: \t\tactionReason = plans.ResourceInstanceReplaceBecauseTainted\n1154: \t}\n1155: \n1156: \t// If we plan to change the sensitivity on some portion of the value, this\n1157: \t// is an Update action even when the values are otherwise equal.\n1158: \t//\n1159: \t// The marks should be normalized by being applied to the new value. We\n1160: \t// don't try to compare the marks we had collected from the config and\n1161: \t// schema, because the act of applying marks to a value may result in\n1162: \t// slightly different marks. For example marks within a set transfer to the\n1163: \t// entire set, and are not saved on the individual elements.\n1164: \tif action == plans.NoOp && !valueMarksEqual(plannedNewVal, priorVal) {\n1165: \t\taction = plans.Update\n1166: \t}\n1167: \n1168: \t// As a special case, if we have a previous diff (presumably from the plan\n1169: \t// phases, whereas we're now in the apply phase) and it was for a replace,\n1170: \t// we've already deleted the original object from state by the time we\n1171: \t// get here and so we would've ended up with a _create_ action this time,\n1172: \t// which we now need to paper over to get a result consistent with what\n1173: \t// we originally intended.\n1174: \tif plannedChange != nil {\n1175: \t\tprevChange := *plannedChange\n1176: \t\tif prevChange.Action.IsReplace() && action == plans.Create {\n1177: \t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] plan: %s treating Create change as %s change to match with earlier plan\", n.Addr, prevChange.Action)\n1178: \t\t\taction = prevChange.Action\n1179: \t\t\tpriorVal = prevChange.Before\n1180: \t\t}\n1181: \t}\n1182: \n1183: \t// Call post-refresh hook\n1184: \tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\n1185: \t\treturn h.PostDiff(n.HookResourceIdentity(), addrs.NotDeposed, action, priorVal, plannedNewVal)\n1186: \t}))\n1187: \tif diags.HasErrors() {\n1188: \t\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags\n1189: \t}\n1190: \n1191: \t// Update our return plan\n1192: \tplan := &plans.ResourceInstanceChange{\n1193: \t\tAddr:         n.Addr,\n1194: \t\tPrevRunAddr:  n.prevRunAddr(ctx),\n1195: \t\tPrivate:      plannedPrivate,\n1196: \t\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider,\n1197: \t\tChange: plans.Change{\n1198: \t\t\tAction: action,\n1199: \t\t\tBefore: priorVal,\n1200: \t\t\t// Pass the marked planned value through in our change\n1201: \t\t\t// to propogate through evaluation.\n1202: \t\t\t// Marks will be removed when encoding.\n1203: \t\t\tAfter:           plannedNewVal,\n1204: \t\t\tGeneratedConfig: n.generatedConfigHCL,\n1205: \t\t},\n1206: \t\tActionReason:    actionReason,\n1207: \t\tRequiredReplace: reqRep,\n1208: \t}\n1209: \n1210: \t// Update our return state\n1211: \tstate := &states.ResourceInstanceObject{\n1212: \t\t// We use the special \"planned\" status here to note that this\n1213: \t\t// object's value is not yet complete. Objects with this status\n1214: \t\t// cannot be used during expression evaluation, so the caller\n1215: \t\t// must _also_ record the returned change in the active plan,\n1216: \t\t// which the expression evaluator will use in preference to this\n1217: \t\t// incomplete value recorded in the state.\n1218: \t\tStatus:  states.ObjectPlanned,\n1219: \t\tValue:   plannedNewVal,\n1220: \t\tPrivate: plannedPrivate,\n1221: \t}\n1222: \n1223: \treturn plan, state, deferred, keyData, diags\n1224: }\n1225: \n1226: func (n *NodeAbstractResource) processIgnoreChanges(prior, config cty.Value, schema *configschema.Block) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n1227: \t// ignore_changes only applies when an object already exists, since we\n1228: \t// can't ignore changes to a thing we've not created yet.\n1229: \tif prior.IsNull() {\n1230: \t\treturn config, nil\n1231: \t}\n1232: \n1233: \tignoreChanges := traversalsToPaths(n.Config.Managed.IgnoreChanges)\n1234: \tignoreAll := n.Config.Managed.IgnoreAllChanges\n1235: \n1236: \tif len(ignoreChanges) == 0 && !ignoreAll {\n1237: \t\treturn config, nil\n1238: \t}\n1239: \n1240: \tif ignoreAll {\n1241: \t\t// Legacy providers need up to clean up their invalid plans and ensure\n1242: \t\t// no changes are passed though, but that also means making an invalid\n1243: \t\t// config with computed values. In that case we just don't supply a\n1244: \t\t// schema and return the prior val directly.\n1245: \t\tif schema == nil {\n1246: \t\t\treturn prior, nil\n1247: \t\t}\n1248: \n1249: \t\t// If we are trying to ignore all attribute changes, we must filter\n1250: \t\t// computed attributes out from the prior state to avoid sending them\n1251: \t\t// to the provider as if they were included in the configuration.\n1252: \t\tret, _ := cty.Transform(prior, func(path cty.Path, v cty.Value) (cty.Value, error) {\n1253: \t\t\tattr := schema.AttributeByPath(path)\n1254: \t\t\tif attr != nil && attr.Computed && !attr.Optional {\n1255: \t\t\t\treturn cty.NullVal(v.Type()), nil\n1256: \t\t\t}\n1257: \n1258: \t\t\treturn v, nil\n1259: \t\t})\n1260: \n1261: \t\treturn ret, nil\n1262: \t}\n1263: \n1264: \tif prior.IsNull() || config.IsNull() {\n1265: \t\t// Ignore changes doesn't apply when we're creating for the first time.\n1266: \t\t// Proposed should never be null here, but if it is then we'll just let it be.\n1267: \t\treturn config, nil\n1268: \t}\n1269: \n1270: \tret, diags := processIgnoreChangesIndividual(prior, config, ignoreChanges)\n1271: \n1272: \treturn ret, diags\n1273: }\n1274: \n1275: // Convert the hcl.Traversal values we get form the configuration to the\n1276: // cty.Path values we need to operate on the cty.Values\n1277: func traversalsToPaths(traversals []hcl.Traversal) []cty.Path {\n1278: \tpaths := make([]cty.Path, len(traversals))\n1279: \tfor i, traversal := range traversals {\n1280: \t\tpath := traversalToPath(traversal)\n1281: \t\tpaths[i] = path\n1282: \t}\n1283: \treturn paths\n1284: }\n1285: \n1286: func traversalToPath(traversal hcl.Traversal) cty.Path {\n1287: \tpath := make(cty.Path, len(traversal))\n1288: \tfor si, step := range traversal {\n1289: \t\tswitch ts := step.(type) {\n1290: \t\tcase hcl.TraverseRoot:\n1291: \t\t\tpath[si] = cty.GetAttrStep{\n1292: \t\t\t\tName: ts.Name,\n1293: \t\t\t}\n1294: \t\tcase hcl.TraverseAttr:\n1295: \t\t\tpath[si] = cty.GetAttrStep{\n1296: \t\t\t\tName: ts.Name,\n1297: \t\t\t}\n1298: \t\tcase hcl.TraverseIndex:\n1299: \t\t\tpath[si] = cty.IndexStep{\n1300: \t\t\t\tKey: ts.Key,\n1301: \t\t\t}\n1302: \t\tdefault:\n1303: \t\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"unsupported traversal step %#v\", step))\n1304: \t\t}\n1305: \t}\n1306: \treturn path\n1307: }\n1308: \n1309: func processIgnoreChangesIndividual(prior, config cty.Value, ignoreChangesPath []cty.Path) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n1310: \ttype ignoreChange struct {\n1311: \t\t// Path is the full path, minus any trailing map index\n1312: \t\tpath cty.Path\n1313: \t\t// Value is the value we are to retain at the above path. If there is a\n1314: \t\t// key value, this must be a map and the desired value will be at the\n1315: \t\t// key index.\n1316: \t\tvalue cty.Value\n1317: \t\t// Key is the index key if the ignored path ends in a map index.\n1318: \t\tkey cty.Value\n1319: \t}\n1320: \tvar ignoredValues []ignoreChange\n1321: \n1322: \t// Find the actual changes first and store them in the ignoreChange struct.\n1323: \t// If the change was to a map value, and the key doesn't exist in the\n1324: \t// config, it would never be visited in the transform walk.\n1325: \tfor _, icPath := range ignoreChangesPath {\n1326: \t\tkey := cty.NullVal(cty.String)\n1327: \t\t// check for a map index, since maps are the only structure where we\n1328: \t\t// could have invalid path steps.\n1329: \t\tlast, ok := icPath[len(icPath)-1].(cty.IndexStep)\n1330: \t\tif ok {\n1331: \t\t\tif last.Key.Type() == cty.String {\n1332: \t\t\t\ticPath = icPath[:len(icPath)-1]\n1333: \t\t\t\tkey = last.Key\n1334: \t\t\t}\n1335: \t\t}\n1336: \n1337: \t\t// The structure should have been validated already, and we already\n1338: \t\t// trimmed the trailing map index. Any other intermediate index error\n1339: \t\t// means we wouldn't be able to apply the value below, so no need to\n1340: \t\t// record this.\n1341: \t\tp, err := icPath.Apply(prior)\n1342: \t\tif err != nil {\n1343: \t\t\tcontinue\n1344: \t\t}\n1345: \t\tc, err := icPath.Apply(config)\n1346: \t\tif err != nil {\n1347: \t\t\tcontinue\n1348: \t\t}\n1349: \n1350: \t\t// If this is a map, it is checking the entire map value for equality\n1351: \t\t// rather than the individual key. This means that the change is stored\n1352: \t\t// here even if our ignored key doesn't change. That is OK since it\n1353: \t\t// won't cause any changes in the transformation, but allows us to skip\n1354: \t\t// breaking up the maps and checking for key existence here too.\n1355: \t\tif !p.RawEquals(c) {\n1356: \t\t\t// there a change to ignore at this path, store the prior value\n1357: \t\t\tignoredValues = append(ignoredValues, ignoreChange{icPath, p, key})\n1358: \t\t}\n1359: \t}\n1360: \n1361: \tif len(ignoredValues) == 0 {\n1362: \t\treturn config, nil\n1363: \t}\n1364: \n1365: \tret, _ := cty.Transform(config, func(path cty.Path, v cty.Value) (cty.Value, error) {\n1366: \t\t// Easy path for when we are only matching the entire value. The only\n1367: \t\t// values we break up for inspection are maps.\n1368: \t\tif !v.Type().IsMapType() {\n1369: \t\t\tfor _, ignored := range ignoredValues {\n1370: \t\t\t\tif path.Equals(ignored.path) {\n1371: \t\t\t\t\treturn ignored.value, nil\n1372: \t\t\t\t}\n1373: \t\t\t}\n1374: \t\t\treturn v, nil\n1375: \t\t}\n1376: \t\t// We now know this must be a map, so we need to accumulate the values\n1377: \t\t// key-by-key.\n1378: \n1379: \t\tif !v.IsNull() && !v.IsKnown() {\n1380: \t\t\t// since v is not known, we cannot ignore individual keys\n1381: \t\t\treturn v, nil\n1382: \t\t}\n1383: \n1384: \t\t// The map values will remain as cty values, so we only need to store\n1385: \t\t// the marks from the outer map itself\n1386: \t\tv, vMarks := v.Unmark()\n1387: \n1388: \t\t// The configMap is the current configuration value, which we will\n1389: \t\t// mutate based on the ignored paths and the prior map value.\n1390: \t\tvar configMap map[string]cty.Value\n1391: \t\tswitch {\n1392: \t\tcase v.IsNull() || v.LengthInt() == 0:\n1393: \t\t\tconfigMap = map[string]cty.Value{}\n1394: \t\tdefault:\n1395: \t\t\tconfigMap = v.AsValueMap()\n1396: \t\t}\n1397: \n1398: \t\tfor _, ignored := range ignoredValues {\n1399: \t\t\tif !path.Equals(ignored.path) {\n1400: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n1401: \t\t\t}\n1402: \n1403: \t\t\tif ignored.key.IsNull() {\n1404: \t\t\t\t// The map address is confirmed to match at this point,\n1405: \t\t\t\t// so if there is no key, we want the entire map and can\n1406: \t\t\t\t// stop accumulating values.\n1407: \t\t\t\treturn ignored.value, nil\n1408: \t\t\t}\n1409: \t\t\t// Now we know we are ignoring a specific index of this map, so get\n1410: \t\t\t// the config map and modify, add, or remove the desired key.\n1411: \n1412: \t\t\t// We also need to create a prior map, so we can check for\n1413: \t\t\t// existence while getting the value, because Value.Index will\n1414: \t\t\t// return null for a key with a null value and for a non-existent\n1415: \t\t\t// key.\n1416: \t\t\tvar priorMap map[string]cty.Value\n1417: \n1418: \t\t\t// We need to drop the marks from the ignored map for handling. We\n1419: \t\t\t// don't need to store these, as we now know the ignored value is\n1420: \t\t\t// only within the map, not the map itself.\n1421: \t\t\tignoredVal, _ := ignored.value.Unmark()\n1422: \n1423: \t\t\tswitch {\n1424: \t\t\tcase ignored.value.IsNull() || ignoredVal.LengthInt() == 0:\n1425: \t\t\t\tpriorMap = map[string]cty.Value{}\n1426: \t\t\tdefault:\n1427: \t\t\t\tpriorMap = ignoredVal.AsValueMap()\n1428: \t\t\t}\n1429: \n1430: \t\t\tkey := ignored.key.AsString()\n1431: \t\t\tpriorElem, keep := priorMap[key]\n1432: \n1433: \t\t\tswitch {\n1434: \t\t\tcase !keep:\n1435: \t\t\t\t// this didn't exist in the old map value, so we're keeping the\n1436: \t\t\t\t// \"absence\" of the key by removing it from the config\n1437: \t\t\t\tdelete(configMap, key)\n1438: \t\t\tdefault:\n1439: \t\t\t\tconfigMap[key] = priorElem\n1440: \t\t\t}\n1441: \t\t}\n1442: \n1443: \t\tvar newVal cty.Value\n1444: \t\tswitch {\n1445: \t\tcase len(configMap) > 0:\n1446: \t\t\tnewVal = cty.MapVal(configMap)\n1447: \t\tcase v.IsNull():\n1448: \t\t\t// if the config value was null, and no values remain in the map,\n1449: \t\t\t// reset the value to null.\n1450: \t\t\tnewVal = v\n1451: \t\tdefault:\n1452: \t\t\tnewVal = cty.MapValEmpty(v.Type().ElementType())\n1453: \t\t}\n1454: \n1455: \t\tif len(vMarks) > 0 {\n1456: \t\t\tnewVal = newVal.WithMarks(vMarks)\n1457: \t\t}\n1458: \n1459: \t\treturn newVal, nil\n1460: \t})\n1461: \treturn ret, nil\n1462: }\n1463: \n1464: // readDataSource handles everything needed to call ReadDataSource on the provider.\n1465: // A previously evaluated configVal can be passed in, or a new one is generated\n1466: // from the resource configuration.\n1467: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) readDataSource(ctx EvalContext, configVal cty.Value) (cty.Value, *providers.Deferred, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n1468: \tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n1469: \tvar newVal cty.Value\n1470: \tvar deferred *providers.Deferred\n1471: \n1472: \tconfig := *n.Config\n1473: \tdeferralAllowed := ctx.Deferrals().DeferralAllowed()\n1474: \n1475: \tprovider, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n1476: \tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n1477: \tif diags.HasErrors() {\n1478: \t\treturn newVal, deferred, diags\n1479: \t}\n1480: \tschema, _ := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceAddr(n.Addr.ContainingResource().Resource)\n1481: \tif schema == nil {\n1482: \t\t// Should be caught during validation, so we don't bother with a pretty error here\n1483: \t\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\"provider %q does not support data source %q\", n.ResolvedProvider, n.Addr.ContainingResource().Resource.Type))\n1484: \t\treturn newVal, deferred, diags\n1485: \t}\n1486: \n1487: \tmetaConfigVal, metaDiags := n.providerMetas(ctx)\n1488: \tdiags = diags.Append(metaDiags)\n1489: \tif diags.HasErrors() {\n1490: \t\treturn newVal, deferred, diags\n1491: \t}\n1492: \n1493: \t// Unmark before sending to provider, will re-mark before returning\n1494: \tvar pvm []cty.PathValueMarks\n1495: \tconfigVal, pvm = configVal.UnmarkDeepWithPaths()\n1496: \n1497: \tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] readDataSource: Re-validating config for %s\", n.Addr)\n1498: \tvalidateResp := provider.ValidateDataResourceConfig(\n1499: \t\tproviders.ValidateDataResourceConfigRequest{\n1500: \t\t\tTypeName: n.Addr.ContainingResource().Resource.Type,\n1501: \t\t\tConfig:   configVal,\n1502: \t\t},\n1503: \t)\n1504: \tdiags = diags.Append(validateResp.Diagnostics.InConfigBody(config.Config, n.Addr.String()))\n1505: \tif diags.HasErrors() {\n1506: \t\treturn newVal, deferred, diags\n1507: \t}\n1508: \n1509: \t// If we get down here then our configuration is complete and we're read\n1510: \t// to actually call the provider to read the data.\n1511: \tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] readDataSource: %s configuration is complete, so reading from provider\", n.Addr)\n1512: \n1513: \tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\n1514: \t\treturn h.PreApply(n.HookResourceIdentity(), addrs.NotDeposed, plans.Read, cty.NullVal(configVal.Type()), configVal)\n1515: \t}))\n1516: \tif diags.HasErrors() {\n1517: \t\treturn newVal, deferred, diags\n1518: \t}\n1519: \n1520: \tvar resp
